observations.what_customers_say: Customers value advice that does not feel like the provider is forcing the largest package.
observations.decision_relevance: A fit-based proposal can be more trustworthy than an upsell-heavy one.

observations.what_customers_say: Positive review language suggests customers notice whether advice fits their situation.
observations.decision_relevance: Buyers may need to see why a selected package is appropriate, including why alternatives were not chosen.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers value help with applications, forms, and registration complexity.
observations.decision_relevance: Paperwork and process friction can be part of the perceived cost of signing.

observations.what_customers_say: Leasing or finance comparison raises questions about total cost, obligations, and fit.
observations.decision_relevance: A monthly-payment option may solve liquidity but create new questions about value and commitment.

observations.what_customers_say: Help with applications and paperwork appears as a concrete service benefit.
observations.decision_relevance: A customer may be more willing to proceed if the administrative path feels manageable.

cross_source_notes: No-pressure advice appears in damm-solar and other positive review patterns.
Administrative handling overlaps with Sunvitec and GSMSolar full-service themes.
caveats: Positive provider-profile reviews may be solicited.
Administrative promises must be backed by the installer's real process.
